DENVER, Col, April l9.-General
Sherman Bell and Captain Buckley
Wells of troop A arrived from Tellu-
ride yesterday and it was learned for
the first time that both had formerly
officially declined service in the SUI
preme court in the writ of habeas
corpus issued for the president, Meyer,
of the Western Federation of Miners.


Generals Bell and Wells went into
long conference over the matter with
Governor Peabody at the conclusion
of which that executive gave symp-
toms of reversing himself in the mat
ter of ordering Bell to produce Meyer.


Governor Peabody will probably fol-
lw one of two courses, either refuse to
order Bell in the matter OF ask the
supreme court to so modify its orders
that the presence of Meyer will not
be required when the case comes up
Thursday.
